summary: O'Neil announces the upcoming 2018 Women's March, noting she discovered it through the telltale sign of pink yarn shortages at craft stores. She urges readers to participate and marks the date while connecting the march's importance to the ongoing #MeToo movement. The post links to revelations about radio host John Hockenberry's systematic harassment and discrimination against women of color colleagues as evidence of why such activism remains necessary.
key_points: The 2018 Women's March will take place on January 20th in cities across the country
The pink pussy hat phenomenon has become culturally significant enough to cause yarn shortages
The #MeToo movement makes the march more timely than ever
Hockenberry case illustrates intersection of sexual harassment and racial discrimination
money_quotes: I found out there's going to be another Women's March by – you guessed it – going to a yarn store and finding there'd been a run on pink yarn.
There's never been a better time for this, I'm sorry to say.
key_insight: Grassroots movements like the Women's March gain cultural traction that becomes visible even in unexpected places like yarn store inventory.
